folio.label: 132r
folio.l: He to his hous is gon , witú sorweful herte
He seetú he may nat , froo his deetú a sterte
Hym semed that he felt , his hert colde
Vp to the heuene , hise hondes he gan holde
And on hise knees bare , he sette hi~ doun
And in his rauyngö , seide his orisoun
For werray woo , out of his wyt he breide
He nyste what he spak , but thus he seide
Witú pitous herte , his pleynt hatú he bygonne
Vn to the goddes , and first vn to the Sonne
¢ He seide Appollo , god and gouernour
Of euäy plaunte , herbe , Tree and flour
That ©euest after , thy declynaciouö
To eche of hem , his tyme and his sesoun
As thyn herberwe chaungetú , lowe or heigúe
Lord Phebus , cast thy mäcyable eye
On wrecched Aurelie , whicú that am but lorn~
Loo lord my lady , hatú my detú I sworn~
Witú outen gilt , but thyn benygnetee
Vp on my dedly hert , haue som pitee
For wel I woot , lord Phebus , if ©ow list
ñe moun me helpen , saue my lady best
Now vouche saf , that I may ©ow deuyse
How that I may be holpen , and in what wyse
¢ ñoure blisful Suster, lucyna the shene

note.notetxt: lucyna337 ".i. luna" interlinear gloss, it is not a correction.MP/ODR

folio.l: That of the See , is chief goddes and quene
Thougú Neptunë , haue deitee in the See
ñet emperesse a bouen hi~ , is she
ñe knowen wel lord , that rigút as hire desire
Is to be quyked and ligúted , of ©oure fyre
For whicú she folwetú ©ow , ful besily
Rigút to the See , desiretú naturely
To folwen hire , as she that is goddesse
Bothe in the See , and Ryuers more and lesse
Wherfore lord Phebus , this is my requeste
Do this myracle , or do myn herte breste
That now next , at this appossicioun~
Whicú in the signe shal be , of the leoun
As preietú hire , so gret a flood to brynge
That fyue fadme at the leste , it ouä spénge
The heieste Rokke , in armoryk Brytayne
And late this flode , endure ©eres tweyne
Thanne certes to my lady , may I seye
Haldetú ©oure heste , the Rokkes ben a weye
¢ Lord Phebus , do this myracle for me
